question: What conditions can be used in the extended form of depends_on?

options.text: service_healthy — wait until healthcheck passes
service_started — enough that the container is started
service_completed_successfully — wait for successful completion (exit code 0)
service_ready — wait until the service opens a port
options.explanation: The service_ready condition doesn't exist. To check port readiness, use healthcheck with curl or wget

question: What happens when using condition: service_healthy if the target service has no healthcheck?

options.text: Docker Compose will throw an error on startup
Compose will skip the check and start the service immediately
options.explanation: Compose strictly validates the healthcheck presence on the target service and won't start the dependent service without it

options.text: The service will be considered always healthy
options.explanation: Compose can't determine health status without a healthcheck and will throw an error

options.text: Compose will automatically add a default healthcheck
options.explanation: Compose doesn't add healthchecks automatically. They must be explicitly defined for each service

question: What are profiles used for in Docker Compose?

options.text: For conditional service startup — e.g., dev tools only during debugging
For limiting CPU and RAM resources for services
options.explanation: Resource limits are configured via deploy.resources.limits, not profiles

options.text: For specifying service startup order
options.explanation: Startup order is determined by depends_on. Profiles determine which services are included

options.text: For selecting a Docker network for each service
options.explanation: Networks are defined via the networks key in service configuration, not profiles

question: How do lists (ports, volumes) behave when merging docker-compose.yml and an override file?

options.text: Lists are concatenated — elements from override are added to existing ones
Override lists completely replace lists from the main file
options.explanation: Lists are appended, not replaced. If the main file has '3000:3000' and override has '9229:9229', both will be present

options.text: Docker Compose merges duplicates and keeps only unique entries
options.explanation: Compose doesn't deduplicate — all elements are concatenated. Duplicating a port will cause 'port already allocated' error

options.text: Only the override file determines the final list
options.explanation: The override file supplements, not replaces. Elements from the main file are preserved

question: Which Compose Watch action is appropriate for package.json changes?

options.text: rebuild — rebuilds the image and recreates the container
sync — copies the file to the container without restart
options.explanation: sync only copies files. Installing new dependencies from package.json requires a full image rebuild

options.text: sync+restart — copies the file and restarts the container
options.explanation: sync+restart will restart the container but won't reinstall dependencies. A full rebuild is needed

options.text: Compose Watch doesn't support tracking package.json
options.explanation: Watch can track any files. For package.json, use action: rebuild
